dataset: Synthetic Viral Meningitis 2024 County Cases and Population Data

pi_items.verified_value: Fulton, Gwinnett, Cobb, DeKalb, and Clayton are real Georgia counties in the Atlanta metropolitan area
pi_items.source: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts, Wikipedia, Library of Congress maps

pi_items.verified_value: CDC surveillance data for 2024 primarily covers meningococcal disease (bacterial meningitis), not viral meningitis. County-level incidence data for viral meningitis is not available in CDC NNDSS or CDC WONDER databases.
pi_items.source: CDC NNDSS (https://wonder.cdc.gov/nndss.html), CDC Meningococcal Disease Surveillance (https://www.cdc.gov/meningococcal/php/surveillance/index.html), CDC Health Alert Network (https://www.cdc.gov/han/2024/han00505.html)

pi_items.metadata_value: Population.csv referenced as input data source
pi_items.verified_value: 2024 population estimates for these Georgia counties are publicly available from U.S. Census Bureau. Fulton County: 1,076,561; Gwinnett County: 979,864; Cobb County: 775,208; DeKalb County: 765,351; Clayton County data available from Census Bureau QuickFacts
pi_items.source: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts (https://www.census.gov/quickfacts/), Data USA, FRED St. Louis Fed

pi_items.verified_value: No public CDC data sources provide county-level viral meningitis case counts for 2024. The verifier.py references a 'ground_truth.json' file, indicating this task uses synthetic test data with predetermined expected results.
pi_items.source: Code analysis of verifier.py, CDC surveillance documentation review

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetic data. The five counties (Fulton, Gwinnett, Cobb, DeKalb, and Clayton) are verified as real Georgia counties in the Atlanta metropolitan area with publicly available 2024 population data ranging from approximately 765,000 to 1.08 million. However, county-level incidence data for Viral Meningitis in 2024 does not exist in public CDC surveillance databases; CDC focuses on meningococcal disease (bacterial), not viral meningitis, and provides only state-level reporting. The verifier references an expected 'ground_truth.json' file containing predetermined correct answers, confirming this is a synthetic task created for training purposes.
